channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: OptinMonster WordPress plugin hacked in CDN supply-chain attack
item.description: WordPress plugins OptinMonster, TrustPulse, and PushEngage have been compromised in a supply-chain attack impacting Awesome Motive-s content distribution network (CDN). [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Cisco fixes SD-WAN vManage flaw exploited in zero-day attacks
item.description: Cisco has released security updates to address a vulnerability in the Catalyst SD-WAN Manager, tracked as CVE-2026-20262, that was exploited in attacks to escalate to root privileges. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: China-Nexus Actor Spy on US Researchers Undetected for a Year
item.description: Google discovered and disrupted the sprawling campaign, which stole RedCAP credentials to target numerous institutions and exfiltrate sensitive data....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Most CISOs Report Pressure to Bury Bad Security News
item.description: Executive leaders may not be saying it aloud, but business objectives and priorities don't always promote timely disclosures....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: LiteLLM Vulnerability Chain Lets Low-Privilege Users Take Over AI Gateway Servers
item.description:
A default low-privilege account on a LiteLLM proxy can climb to full admin and run code on the server by chaining three vulnerabilities, researchers at Obsidian Security disclosed
LiteLLM is a widely...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Council of Europe investigates ShinyHunters data breach claims
item.description: The Council of Europe, the continent's oldest intergovernmental body, is probing claims of a data breach made by the ShinyHunters extortion group over the weekend. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: FBI: Fraudsters use couriers to steal money in crypto scams
item.description: The U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) warned that criminals are using couriers to collect money from victims of cryptocurrency investment scams, also known as pig butchering or romance baitin...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: One-Click Microsoft 365 Copilot Flaw Could Have Let Attackers Steal Emails, Files, and MFA Codes
item.description:
A single click on a trusted Microsoft link could have let an attacker pull emails, calendar details, and indexed files out of Microsoft 365 Copilot Enterprise Search.
Researchers at Varonis Threat La...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: The Beginning of the End of Social Engineering
item.description: AI-native operating systems are shifting the responsibility to stay vigilant against social engineering cyberattacks from the user onto the system itself....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Vibe coders are gonna vibe code: How CISOs are tackling code sprawl
item.description: Employees are increasingly building automations, agents, and apps with AI tools outside traditional security oversight. Tines explores how CISOs are handling AI-driven code sprawl, shadow tooling, and...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Chinese hackers breach REDCap servers, steal medical research
item.description: A China-linked espionage campaign targeted exposed REDCap servers to deploy the InfiniteRed malware and steal sensitive data from a medical institution in North America. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Chrome 0-Day, UniFi Exploits, macOS Stealers, VPN Flaw and More
item.description:
Stuff broke again. Not in a movie way. An old tool was left exposed. An abandoned package was abused. A deprecated feature was still running in prod.
This week is the same lesson in a new form: phish...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New attack turned Microsoft 365 Copilot into 1-click data theft tool
item.description: A critical vulnerability chain dubbed SearchLeak in Microsoft 365 Copilot Enterprise could allow attackers to steal sensitive data from a target's mailbox, OneDrive, or SharePoint account through a sp...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Infinite Campus data breach affects 137,000 school staff accounts
item.description: The ShinyHunters extortion gang stole personal information from more than 137,000 school staff accounts in a Salesforce data theft attack that targeted the widely used Infinite Campus K-12 student inf...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: US Cracks Down on Anthropic AI Models Amid Abuse Concerns
item.description: Anthropic abruptly suspended all access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 after receiving an export control directive that banned foreign nationals from using the technology....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Webinar: How behavioral AI stops phishing and account takeovers
item.description: Modern phishing, BEC, and account takeover attacks increasingly bypass traditional email defenses and create operational strain for security teams. This webinar explores how behavioral AI can help aut...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: The Onboarding Password Mistake That Creates Unnecessary Risk
item.description:
Employee onboarding is a busy time for IT teams. New starters need devices, accounts, access permissions, and passwords, all delivered within a tight timeframe.
That usually means sharing a temporary...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: 152 Chrome Wallpaper Extensions with 105K Installs Linked to Adware and Fake Traffic
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a network of 152 Google Chrome extensions that act as new tab live wallpaper add-ons to distribute a potentially unwanted program (PUP) family.
The cluster s...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Popular WordPress Plugin Scripts Tampered to Plant Hidden Backdoors on Sites
item.description:
An attacker tampered with trusted JavaScript files used by WordPress sites running PushEngage, OptinMonster, and TrustPulse, turning those files into a way to break into the sites.
When a site admini...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Sniper Dz Scams Target MENA Users via Fake Facebook Offers and Browser Alerts
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of fraudulent activity targeting users across the Middle East and North Africa by employing various fraudulent Facebook accounts impersonating politici...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Palo Alto Warns of Active Exploitation of PAN-OS GlobalProtect VPN Flaw
item.description: Palo Alto Networks has revealed that it has observed "active exploitation" of a recently disclosed PAN-OS vulnerability by an unknown threat actor to obtain unauthorized access to GlobalProtect portal...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: FBI disrupts massive AI-powered phishing service using a million URLs
item.description: In a coordinated effort, the FBI, working with Google and Black Lotus Labs, has dismantled a massive Chinese phishing-as-a-service operation called Outsider Enterprise with thousands of phishing websi...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Ex-school district employee jailed for hacks on former employer
item.description: A former IT employee at an Iowa school district was sentenced to 21 months in prison after conducting a prolonged cyberattack against the former employer that disrupted classroom operations, deleted ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Chinese hackers hijack auth flow, spy on isolated network for a decade
item.description: Chinese hackers took control of a target organization's authentication stack and maintained persistence for 10 years, with full visibility into the administrative activity. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Critical Splunk Enterprise Flaw Lets Attackers Run Code Without Authentication
item.description:
Splunk has released security updates to address a critical security flaw in Splunk Enterprise that could be exploited to conduct unauthenticated file operations and even remote code execution.
The vu...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: US Gov asks Anthropic to ban 'foreign national' access to Fable, Mythos
item.description: The US government has ordered Anthropic to block all foreign nationals from accessing Fable 5 and Mythos 5, forcing the company to suspend both models worldwide. Anthropic is complying but disputes th...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: U.S. Orders Anthropic to Suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Access for Foreign Nationals
item.description: Anthropic said on Friday it will "abruptly disable" its most advanced artificial intelligence (AI) models, Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, for all users after the U.S. government ordered it to suspend ac...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: ShinyHunters Uses Oracle Zero-Day to Rampage Higher Ed
item.description: A major bug in Oracle's ERP software disproportionately affected American universities, and hackers have capitalized by stealing gobs of data....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Maine disables data breach notification portal after fake disclosures
item.description: Maine has taken its public data breach reporting portal offline after fraudulent breach disclosures were published on the state's website, prompting a review of procedures to prevent abuse in the futu...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked to Deploy Infostealer and eBPF Rootkit
item.description:
Attackers took over more than 400 packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR) this week and rewrote their build scripts to install a credential stealer on any machine that built them.
The malware is a...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
